title: oxycoccus palustris var. ovalifolius
intro: Oxycoccus palustris var. ovalifolius, also known as bog cranberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to bogs and acidic soils in the Northern Hemisphere. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Oxycoccus palustris var. ovalifolius include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are often used to make teas, capsules, or topical creams.
